name: Alex the API-First DevOps Engineer
summary: A Senior DevOps Engineer at a 50-200 person fintech company who approaches the project management tool as an integration platform. Long API doc sessions, webhook configs, and Terraform work reveal an automation-first mindset.
goals: Automate all project state transitions via API to eliminate manual ticket updates
Integrate deeply with GitHub CI/CD so PR merges auto-close tasks
Set up webhook-driven Slack alerts for real-time status visibility
Provision the workspace via Terraform for reproducible infrastructure
pains: GraphQL endpoint has rough edges and schema inconsistencies that break automation
Webhook configuration lacks documentation for edge cases and retry behavior
Team invite workflow forces manual UI steps that interrupt automation sessions
motivations: Reducing toil — every manual step is a future incident
Proving infrastructure-as-code extends to the project management layer
objections: Unstable GraphQL schema without versioning cannot be trusted in production pipelines
Immature Terraform provider means maintaining a custom fork
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges. Plans to improve the schema?
If I can't manage it in Terraform, it doesn't exist in our infrastructure.
I don't want to click anything. Give me an API endpoint.

journey_stages.mindset: Scanning API quality signals before investing integration time

source_evidence.claim: Senior DevOps Engineer at 50-200 person fintech
GraphQL has rough edges breaking automation
Automate all project state transitions via API
Integrate with GitHub CI/CD for auto-close on PR merge
Reducing toil drives long API setup sessions
Infrastructure-as-code mindset shown by Terraform usage
Unstable GraphQL schema is a production trust issue
Terraform provider immaturity forces custom maintenance
